summary: postinstall exfiltrates npm auth tokens and host files to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that reads npm credentials from multiple.npmrc paths (/home/node/.npmrc, /setup/.npmrc, /setup/node/.npmrc, /usr/lib/node_modules/npm/.npmrc), recursively greps for `_authToken=` tokens and `HTB{...}` flag patterns across /setup and /home/node, reads /etc/hosts and /proc/1/mountinfo, executes /readflag, base64-encodes the collected output, and PUTs it over plain HTTP to the hardcoded bare IP 154.57.164.64 on port 32271 across three sink paths (/api/modules/ECT-987654, /api/modules/ECT-839201, /api/modules/ECT-472839). Execution is gated by environment fingerprinting: aborts under CI/GitHub Actions and common mirror hostnames, and only fires when the hostname matches a 12-hex-character pattern and cwd contains '/app' — a Docker-container target fingerprint and sandbox-evasion shape. Captured npm tokens allow the attacker to publish malicious versions under the installer's npm identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
